 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ehsz","title":"Implement spam prevention for community content","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-7g3z.

## Task
Implement spam prevention mechanisms for user-generated content.

## Why This Matters
- Protect quality of Swap Meet
- Prevent abuse of platform
- Maintain user trust
- Legal protection

## Spam Prevention Layers

### 1. Rate Limiting
- Max prompts per hour: 10
- Max prompts per day: 50
- Burst protection
- Per-user limits

### 2. Content Filtering
- Block known spam patterns
- URL spam detection
- Excessive capitalization
- Repeated characters
- Known bad words

### 3. New User Restrictions
- First 24 hours: limited posting
- First 7 days: moderation queue
- Earn trust over time

### 4. Automated Detection
- AI-based spam scoring (optional)
- Pattern matching
- Similarity to known spam
- Link density analysis

### 5. User Reporting
- Report spam button
- Quick review queue
- User reputation impact

### 6. Account Health Score
- Based on: reports, quality, engagement
- Low score = more scrutiny
- High score = less friction

## Implementation

### Content Check Service
```typescript
interface SpamCheckResult {
  isSpam: boolean;
  confidence: number;
  reasons: string[];
  requiresReview: boolean;
}

async function checkContent(content: string, userId: string): Promise<SpamCheckResult>
```

### Moderation Queue
- Content flagged for review
- Admin approve/reject
- Learn from decisions

## Database
```typescript
export const contentFlags = pgTable("content_flags", {
  id: uuid("id").primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  contentId: text("content_id").notNull(),
  contentType: text("content_type").notNull(),
  flagType: text("flag_type").notNull(), // spam, abuse, copyright
  reportedBy: uuid("reported_by").references(() => userProfiles.id),
  status: text("status").default("pending"),
  reviewedBy: uuid("reviewed_by"),
  createdAt: timestamp("created_at").defaultNow(),
});
```

## Files to Create
- src/lib/moderation/spam-check.ts
- src/lib/moderation/content-filter.ts
- src/app/api/moderation/report/route.ts
- src/components/moderation/report-button.tsx

## Success Criteria
- Spam blocked effectively
- False positives low
- Legitimate users unaffected
- Easy to review/appeal

## UX & Fairness Additions
- Provide transparent feedback when content is flagged (reason + appeal path).
- Add soft-block state for borderline content (visible to author only).
- Log moderation decisions for model tuning and dispute handling.

## Test Coverage
- Unit: spam scoring thresholds + pattern filters
- Integration: moderation queue creation + review actions
- E2E: jeffreysprompts_premium-gx6z (spam + reporting + moderation)

-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ezv2","title":"Implement changelog and what's new feature","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-xj4e.

## Task
Implement a changelog to communicate new features and updates to users.

## Why This Matters
- Users know about new features
- Reduces "I didn't know you could do that"
- Shows product is actively developed
- Marketing opportunity

## Components

### 1. Changelog Page
- /changelog - Public page
- Chronological list of updates
- Categories: New, Improved, Fixed
- Date for each entry
- SEO-friendly

### 2. What's New Modal
- Shown to users after update
- Highlights major changes
- Dismissible (remember dismissal)
- Link to full changelog

### 3. Update Badge
- Small badge on nav item
- "1 new update" indicator
- Clears when viewed

### 4. Email Digest (optional)
- Monthly changelog email
- Major updates only
- For engaged users

## Changelog Entry Format
```markdown
## March 2026

### New
- **Collections Sharing**: Share entire collections with a link
- **Dark Mode**: Toggle between light and dark themes

### Improved
- Search is now 2x faster
- Better mobile navigation

### Fixed
- Fixed issue with export failing for large collections
- Corrected billing date display
```

## Database
```typescript
export const changelogEntries = pgTable("changelog_entries", {
  id: uuid("id").primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  version: text("version"), // optional semver
  title: text("title").notNull(),
  content: text("content").notNull(), // markdown
  category: text("category").notNull(), // new, improved, fixed
  publishedAt: timestamp("published_at"),
  createdAt: timestamp("created_at").defaultNow(),
});

// Track which users have seen which entries
export const changelogViews = pgTable("changelog_views", {
  userId: uuid("user_id").references(() => userProfiles.id),
  lastSeenEntryId: uuid("last_seen_entry_id"),
  seenAt: timestamp("seen_at").defaultNow(),
});
```

## Files to Create
- src/app/(public)/changelog/page.tsx
- src/components/changelog/whats-new-modal.tsx
- src/components/changelog/update-badge.tsx
- content/changelog/*.mdx
- src/app/api/changelog/route.ts

## Success Criteria
- Changelog updated regularly
- Users notified of changes
- Easy to maintain
- SEO-friendly

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-f296","title":"Implement cookie consent and GDPR banner","description":"## Origin
Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-6754. Keep both repos in sync.

## Task
Implement cookie consent banner for GDPR/CCPA compliance.

## Why This Matters
- Legal requirement in EU (GDPR)
- Legal requirement in California (CCPA)
- User privacy expectations
- Avoid potential fines

## Requirements

### Cookie Categories
1. **Essential** (always on)
   - Authentication
   - Security
   - Basic functionality

2. **Analytics** (optional)
   - Google Analytics
   - Usage tracking
   - Performance monitoring

3. **Marketing** (optional)
   - Advertising
   - Retargeting
   - Social media pixels

### Consent Banner
- Shown on first visit
- Clear explanation of cookies
- Accept All / Customize / Reject All
- Link to cookie policy
- Remember preference

### Cookie Settings
- Accessible from footer
- Per-category toggles
- Save preferences
- Withdraw consent anytime

### Implementation
- Don't load tracking scripts until consent
- Store preference in cookie/localStorage
- Respect Do Not Track header
- Log consent for compliance

## Technical Implementation
```typescript
// Cookie consent state
interface CookieConsent {
  essential: true; // always true
  analytics: boolean;
  marketing: boolean;
  consentedAt: string;
  version: string; // consent policy version
}
```

### Conditional Script Loading
```tsx
// Only load GA if analytics consent given
{cookieConsent.analytics && (
  <Script src="https://www.googletagmanager.com/gtag/js" />
)}
```

## Files to Create
- src/components/consent/cookie-banner.tsx
- src/components/consent/cookie-settings.tsx
- src/lib/consent/cookie-consent.ts
- src/hooks/use-cookie-consent.ts
- src/app/(public)/cookies/page.tsx (Cookie Policy)

## Success Criteria
- Banner shows on first visit
- Consent respected
- Scripts load conditionally
- Settings accessible
- Preference persists

## Privacy Additions
- Respect Global Privacy Control (GPC) and DNT headers by defaulting analytics/marketing to off.
- Persist consent server-side (optional) for compliance audits; store consentVersion + timestamp.
- Gate analytics event queue until consent granted; flush only after opt-in.
- Show banner only where required (geo/IP or locale-based), but always allow manual settings link.
